description:
README known-limitation #1 (README.md:300-313): the `:baseline_red` guard rail does NOT trip in production — coverage's ExUnit.run/0 drains ExUnit.Server before baseline runs, so baseline reports failures: 0 regardless of whether cited tests pass.

Important nuance (verified): mutation kill/survive classification is UNAFFECTED — the mutation phase re-registers each module via ExUnit.Server.add_module/2 before every per-site run. So results aren't corrupted; the tool just won't WARN you if your suite is already red.

Previously tracked as mutagen-wrd.37 (now CLOSED but the limitation still ships). Decide: actually fix the guard rail, or make the limitation much louder (e.g. CLI warning banner). Shipping a mutation tool whose 'is your baseline green?' check is documented-broken will generate immediate issues.

Acceptance: either baseline_red fires correctly on a red suite, OR a prominent runtime warning is emitted.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED

Investigation finding: the `:baseline_red` guard rail is NOT broken in production — README known-limitation #1 was STALE documentation describing pre-fix behavior. The mutagen-wrd.37/38 re-registration fix (each phase calls `ExUnit.Server.add_module/2` before its own `ExUnit.run/0`) actually works.

Ground-truth proof (standalone BEAM, no parent ExUnit run — exact `mix mutagen` condition): coverage's `ExUnit.run/0` drains the server (reports failures:1), baseline re-registers + runs, baseline reports failures:1 → guard fires. Verified under the real `Code.require_file/1` cache hazard (cached no-op) the README cites.

Chose the guard-fires route (lower risk than adding a runtime warning, since the guard already satisfies acceptance). Deliverables:

1. test/mutagen_ex/baseline_red_guard_test.exs — NEW production-condition regression test driving the REAL ExUnit/ExUnit.Server (the existing baseline_test.exs uses an ExUnitFake that can't observe the drain hazard; the e2e baseline_red_scenario test exists but is gated behind :e2e_slow, excluded from default `mix test`). Red case asserts :baseline_red fires after a prior run drains the registry; green control asserts no false-positive. Verified it CATCHES the regression: disabling Baseline's re-registration makes exactly the red test fail (returns {:ok, failed: 0} — the documented silent-pass bug).
2. test/fixtures/baseline_red_guard/{red,green}_cited_test.exs — cited fixture modules, tagged :mutagen_baseline_red_guard.
3. test/test_helper.exs — excludes :mutagen_baseline_red_guard from the default run so the deliberately-red fixture isn't a top-level failure.
4. README.md — replaced the false known-limitation #1 with an accurate 'Baseline guard rail' section (guard works, explains the re-registration, points at the tests, notes the old manual-mix-test workaround is no longer needed). Renumbered remaining limitation.

Mutation kill/survive classification untouched (ticket nuance preserved).

Verification: `make smoke` passes (compile --warnings-as-errors + tests). Full suite 642 tests, 0 failures, stable across seeds 0/7/42. mix format clean.

Out of scope / flagged: test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_5_baseline_red.json has PRE-EXISTING golden drift (the abort `details` block gained `message`/`warnings` from the wrd.39.2 details-threading work on main, unrelated to this ticket). The e2e scenario's baseline-red *assertions* (aborted/abort_reason/baseline.failed) all pass; only the byte-equal golden compare drifts. Left untouched rather than silently regen — orchestrator may want a separate golden-refresh.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 5 (README.md, test/test_helper.exs, test/mutagen_ex/baseline_red_guard_test.exs, test/fixtures/baseline_red_guard/red_cited_test.exs, test/fixtures/baseline_red_guard/green_cited_test.exs)
- Files outside reasonable allowed surface (README.md, test/**): 0. No lib/** product-code changes.

Semantic checks:
- Acceptance met (guard fires on red suite, demonstrated by a test that catches the regression): yes. test/mutagen_ex/baseline_red_guard_test.exs:95-134 is the red case. It (1) registers the red fixture and drains ExUnit.Server with a nested ExUnit.run/0 (mirrors the coverage-phase drain the README cited), then (2) calls the REAL Baseline.run/1 with test_modules: [{@red_module, @module_cfg}]. Baseline.run/1's register_test_modules (lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex:175-181) reads :test_modules and re-registers via server.add_module/2 against the default real ExUnit.Server seam. Concrete failure mode caught: if re-registration were removed, the post-drain registry is empty, baseline's ExUnit.run/0 returns failed: 0 => {:ok, ...} (the documented silent-pass bug), so the assert {:error, :baseline_red, details} at line 130 would fail. The green control (line 136-154) guards against re-registration manufacturing false positives. Test setup uses the production default seams (real ExUnit / ExUnit.Server), not the ExUnitFake used elsewhere, so the drain hazard is genuinely exercised.
- README change accurate / no self-contradiction: yes. New 'Baseline guard rail' section states the guard works, explains the re-registration (wrd.37/38), points at the regression test + :baseline_red_scenario e2e, and the blockquote correctly frames the old 'did not trip' limitation as fixed. Old false known-limitation #1 removed, remaining limitation renumbered. Internally consistent.
- Golden drift file absent from diff: yes. test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_5_baseline_red.json is NOT in git diff 196bb8c..HEAD.
- Project verification (make smoke): pass. compile --warnings-as-errors clean; 642 tests, 0 failures (21 excluded).

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory): The deliberately-red fixture is correctly tagged :mutagen_baseline_red_guard and excluded from the default run via test_helper.exs, so it never surfaces as a spurious top-level failure; the guard test re-includes the tag in its nested runs. Pre-existing golden drift on end_to_end_scenario_5_baseline_red.json (from wrd.39.2) was correctly left untouched and is gated behind :e2e_slow.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: 033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3.jsonl

Reasoning: The premise inversion holds. The guard fires because Baseline.register_test_modules (lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex:175-184) calls server.add_module/2 for each :test_modules entry before run_exunit, repopulating the registry that the prior ExUnit.run/0 drained — and production wires this via phase_baseline threading test_modules: TestModuleDiscovery.discover(...) into Baseline.run/1 (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:835). I did NOT trust the trace: I temporarily neutered register_test_modules and ran baseline_red_guard_test.exs — the red case (line 95) failed with MatchError on exactly {:ok, %{failed: 0, ...}}, the documented silent-pass bug; the green control still passed; then I restored lib/ (git diff lib/ clean). The test uses production default seams (no :ex_unit/:ex_unit_server override in the input map; MutagenEx.Test.ExUnit and .ExUnitServer are pure defdelegate to real ExUnit/ExUnit.Server), not the ExUnitFake — so the real drain hazard is exercised. e2e :baseline_red_scenario (end_to_end_test.exs:706) covers the standalone-pipeline condition asserting aborted/abort_reason=baseline_red through production Baseline. Scope: git diff 196bb8c..HEAD = 5 files (README.md + test/**), 0 lib/** product changes. The red fixture is excluded from default mix test via :mutagen_baseline_red_guard in test_helper.exs (full suite 642 tests, 0 failures, 21 excluded — no spurious top-level failure) and the guard test re-includes the tag in its nested runs. New README 'Baseline guard rail' section is accurate and internally consistent with the code (both coverage and baseline re-register) and renumbering left one clean limitation.

APPROVED (orchestrator). VERIFY: PASS + AUDIT: APPROVE (auditor independently neutered Baseline.register_test_modules and confirmed the red test fails with the documented silent-pass MatchError, then restored lib/). Merged --ff-only into epic/hcs. Epic gates green: smoke 642/0, mix format clean, deps.unlock clean. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: Fix or escalate baseline_red guard rail
